reasoning: The question asks if there will be more than ten times the baseline number of protests (meaning at least 27 protests, based on the reference value of 2.667) in Comoros from February 6 to March 7, 2026. Given the forecast date of March 1, 2026, most of this 30-day window has already elapsed. Such an explosion in protests (a 10x spike) typically occurs only around major triggering events, such as the widely-reported post-election unrest in January 2024. A thorough web search revealed that the most recent parliamentary elections occurred in January 2025 (over a year prior) and there are no news reports or current events indicating any significant protests happening in February 2026. With over three-quarters of the observation period already passed without any massive protest activity, the mathematical threshold of a 10x spike is essentially unreachable. The probability is therefore placed at the minimum allowed bound (5%).
